1T Is LETTING THE BARS

KANES MOST DANGEROUS COM


PETITOR.


How can that be, you ask?


Today Portland is crowding Spo-


kane jobbers in every direction. She


is asking for rates which will give


her business far to the eact of what


ever territory Spokane can sell goods


in. Portland jobbers have been IA


vored with rates which have enabled


them to encroach on Seattle territory
and sell at Seattle's door.
At first glance it would look as IF,
under a Washington railroad COM


mission. Portland. in Oregon, would


be shut out.


But all the Portland jobbers would
have to do would be to cross the
river to Vancouver and ship their


goods from that point under the
coast distributing rates set up by a
Washington commission to favor
Washington jobber..


In this way interstate commerce
limitations would be abolished.
Those Spokane shippers who sent


kilograms to Olympia saying they
did not want railroad commission,


have been criticized by politicians.
They should be praised for the eX-
hibition of desperate courage required


to cut loose from the blind followers


of the tall tower theorist, to assert


their right to manage their own bust
ness,


The only answer they have re-
ceived so far from the politicians
and little lawyers at Olympia is that
| these politicians and little lawyers
| were elected on railroad commits
sion platform and that while they
! realize the danger to Spokane job
| bling and manufacturing interests,


THEY DARE NOT DEFEAT THE
COMMISSION BILL FOR FEAR OF


NOT BEING REELECTED!
Yet Nelson w. Durham calls for
a commentator Of these brave,

pended the following dispatch from
